title: BridgeConfig: TOML config parsing with unit tests
description:
## What
Implement BridgeConfig struct in bridge-matrix/src/config.rs with TOML deserialization and unit tests.

## Algorithm
1. Define struct BridgeConfig { relay_url: String, keyfile: String, matrix_homeserver: String, matrix_room_id: String, as_token: String, hs_token: String, bot_localpart: String, bridge_prefix: Option<String> }
2. Implement BridgeConfig::from_toml(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> using std::fs::read_to_string + toml::from_str.
3. DO NOT derive Debug on BridgeConfig (as_token, hs_token are secrets). Implement Display showing only non-secret fields.
4. Validate: relay_url starts with 'ws://' or 'wss://'; matrix_homeserver starts with 'https://'; room_id starts with '!' (Matrix room ID format).
5. Tests: from_toml_defaults_bridge_prefix, validate_rejects_bad_relay_url, validate_rejects_bad_room_id (using tempfile for config files).

## Defensive programming
- Never log as_token or hs_token
- Validate all URL/ID fields before returning Ok
- Required fields must be present (serde deny_unknown_fields would be too strict)
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-bridge-matrix passes; config parsing and validation tests pass
